task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly handles the empty list edge case by returning None.
trace: I sorted the input list to ensure the numbers are in order. If the length of the list is even, I calculated the average of the two middle values by adding them and dividing by 2. If the length of the list is odd, I simply returned the middle value directly. Additionally, I handled an edge case where an empty list is provided by returning None.
